name: Mutual Exclusion
description: A mutual exclusion algorithm for N processes where at most one process is in the critical section at a time.
hints: Should define processes as 1..N, track state as {idle, trying, critical}.

name: Two-Phase Commit
description: A two-phase commit protocol with one coordinator and N participants. The coordinator decides to commit only if all participants vote yes.
hints: Messages: Prepare, VoteYes, VoteNo, Commit, Abort.

name: Dining Philosophers
description: The dining philosophers problem with N philosophers and N forks. Each philosopher picks up both adjacent forks to eat, then puts them down.
hints: Track fork ownership and philosopher states: thinking, hungry, eating.

name: Lamport's Bakery Algorithm
description: Lamport's bakery mutual exclusion algorithm for N processes. Processes take a numbered ticket; lower numbers enter first.
hints: VARIABLES num, flag. Choosing phase before assigning number.

name: Producer-Consumer Queue
description: A bounded FIFO queue with one producer and one consumer. The producer blocks when the queue is full; the consumer blocks when empty.
hints: Queue capacity is a constant K.

name: Raft Leader Election
description: The leader election phase of the Raft consensus algorithm. Servers hold terms and vote for candidates. A candidate wins if it gets a majority of votes.
hints: States: Follower, Candidate, Leader. VARIABLES currentTerm, votedFor, state.

name: Read-Write Lock
description: A read-write lock allowing multiple concurrent readers but exclusive writer access. Writers wait for all readers to finish.
hints: VARIABLES readers, writer_active.

name: Distributed Snapshot (Chandy-Lamport)
description: The Chandy-Lamport distributed snapshot algorithm over a network of N processes with FIFO channels. Each process records its local state and the state of incoming channels.
hints: Send marker messages along each outgoing channel after recording state.

name: Token Ring
description: A token ring network with N nodes. Each node passes a single token clockwise. Only the token holder may send a message.
hints: Nodes 1..N, token position as a variable.

name: Simple Key-Value Store
description: A single-server key-value store supporting Put(k,v) and Get(k) operations. Linearizability: a Get always returns the value of the most recent Put.
hints: Model a finite key space Keys and value space Values.

name: Paxos Single-Decree
description: Single-decree Paxos consensus over N acceptors and M proposers. Once a value is chosen, it is never changed.
hints: Phases: Prepare/Promise, Accept/Accepted. Proposer picks value with highest ballot.

name: Bounded Retransmission Protocol
description: A sender transmits a file in chunks over an unreliable channel. The sender retransmits up to MAX_RETRIES times before giving up.
hints: Model message loss as non-deterministic. Track retransmit count.

name: Transaction Isolation (Snapshot Isolation)
description: A database with snapshot isolation. Transactions read a consistent snapshot. Write-write conflicts cause an abort.
hints: Model transactions as spanning multiple steps with begin/commit/abort.

name: Clock Synchronisation
description: N nodes exchange clock values to synchronise. After one round, all clocks are within epsilon of each other.
hints: Drift modelled as integer offset. Average-based sync.

name: Peterson's Algorithm
description: Peterson's mutual exclusion algorithm for exactly 2 processes.
hints: VARIABLES flag[2], turn.

name: Gossip Protocol
description: A gossip (epidemic) protocol where N nodes periodically share updates. A node that receives an update marks it as 'infected' and spreads it in subsequent rounds.
hints: VARIABLES known, infected sets. Eventual convergence is a liveness property.

name: Simple Allocator
description: A memory allocator managing a fixed pool of N pages. Clients request and release pages. Safety: no page is allocated to two clients simultaneously.
hints: VARIABLES free, allocated. allocated is a function from Client to SUBSET Pages.

name: Publish-Subscribe Broker
description: A single broker with subscribers and publishers. Subscribers register interest in topics. Publishers post messages on topics. The broker delivers each message to all registered subscribers.
hints: Messages arrive in order per topic.

name: Dekker's Algorithm
description: Dekker's mutual exclusion algorithm for 2 processes, the first known correct solution to the problem.
hints: VARIABLES turn, wants. Both safety and liveness should be modelled.

name: Eventually Consistent Counter
description: A distributed grow-only counter (G-Counter CRDT) with N nodes. Each node increments its own slot. Merge takes element-wise max. All nodes eventually agree on the total.
hints: VARIABLES counts[Node][Node]. Global count = sum of max(counts[][n]).
